concerns.text: FR-002 mandates 'CPU-tractable model (CodeLlama-7B or StarCoder... in CPU mode)' but provides no measurable success criterion for 'tractable'. Without a defined time limit or memory cap for the generation step itself, this requirement is unverifiable. Is 10 hours tractable? 100? This must be quantified (e.g., 'generation completes within 30 mins per sample').

FR-009 requires 'complete full analysis... within 6 hours on GitHub Actions free-tier runner'. This is unverifiable as written because the spec does not define the sample size (N) for this 6-hour run. Running on N=10 vs N=50 yields vastly different times. The requirement must specify the N (e.g., 'for N=50 pairs') to be testable.

FR-003 states the system MUST profile 'peak and steady-state memory usage'. It does not define the measurement window or the method for determining 'steady-state' (e.g., 'average memory over the last 10% of execution time' or 'minimum memory observed after GC'). Without a precise algorithm, 'steady-state' is subjective and untestable.

FR-004 mandates 'Wilcoxon signed-rank test' but does not specify the handling of ties or zero-differences (where LLM memory == Human memory). The test implementation must be deterministic; without specifying the tie-breaking rule or exclusion criteria for zero-differences, two implementations may yield different p-values.

SC-005 states 'Sample size adequacy is measured against the minimum N = 50'. This is circular and unmeasurable as a success criterion for the *system*. The system cannot 'measure' its own sample size adequacy against a fixed number; it either collects N=50 or it doesn't. The SC should be rephrased to 'The system MUST collect and process at least 50 valid paired observations' (a functional requirement) or define a statistical power calculation that the system performs.
